meta_title: For Founders: project management to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Stop Abandoning Projects with Project Management
content.seo_description: Stop the cycle of abandoned projects. Learn how to master project management using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship your MVP faster than your competitors.
content.answer_block: The root cause of abandoned projects is a lack of technical guardrails. By integrating Antigravity for structured scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative execution, you transform abstract ideas into a linear, manageable deployment pipeline. Stop building features; start building a revenue-generating machine by shipping incremental, validated code blocks.
content.intro:
Your hard drive is a graveyard of half-baked ideas and abandoned repositories. Every unfinished project is a tax on your mental bandwidth and a direct hit to your potential MRR. The gap between your vision and your finished product isn't a lack of coding skill; it is a failure of systemized execution.

Today, we stop the bleeding. We are moving away from 'coding in the dark' toward a high-velocity, modular development framework. By leveraging Antigravity and Claude Code, we will force your project into a rigid, objective-oriented path that guarantees a finished MVP. You don't need more time; you need better constraints.

steps.title: Define the MVP Scope
steps.description: Strip your project down to its core value proposition. If it doesn't generate revenue or solve the primary pain point, cut it.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Generate a high-level technical requirements document (TRD) for a [INSERT PROJECT IDEA] MVP. Focus on the absolute minimum feature set required to launch and capture user feedback.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate a project structure file named MVP_ROADMAP.md based on the TRD created by Antigravity.'

steps.title: Establish the Scaffold
steps.description: Use Antigravity to build the architectural skeleton. A solid foundation prevents technical debt that kills momentum.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a boilerplate project structure for a SaaS application using [INSERT STACK]. Include authentication, database models, and a simple API route.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize the repository using the scaffold provided by Antigravity and commit the baseline.'

steps.title: Implement Incremental Logic
steps.description: Stop writing massive functions. Use Claude Code to build one atomic feature at a time, ensuring each passes a test suite.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Break down the User Authentication module into 3 micro-tasks: 1. DB Schema, 2. API Endpoint, 3. Frontend Form component.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Implement the User Authentication DB schema provided in the previous step and verify it with a migration.'

steps.title: Automate Quality Gates
steps.description: Never move to the next feature until the previous one is production-ready. This maintains psychological momentum.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a set of unit tests for the authentication module to ensure the build never breaks during future iterations.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Execute all tests and ensure the CI/CD pipeline is configured to block merges on failure.'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Shipping is a feature. Get your code out of your local environment to validate your concept with actual users.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment configuration file for Vercel/Fly.io that optimizes the current build for speed and cost-efficiency.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Deploy the current verified state to the production environment and output the live URL.'

pro_tips: Never start coding until you have a 'Definition of Done' for every single task written down.
Use Claude Code to perform a 'technical debt audit' every 48 hours to prevent feature creep.
If a feature takes longer than 4 hours to build, break it into two smaller sub-tasks immediately.
Prioritize 'UI-first' development to see visual progress, which keeps your founder-motivation high.
Treat your codebase like a product, not a hobby; use version control branches as if you had a team of ten engineers.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, use your live URL to gather 10 user interviews. Feed that qualitative data back into your Antigravity prompts to iterate on the product roadmap.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the framework to stop being a 'project starter' and start being a 'product shipper'. The market doesn't reward perfect code; it rewards completed solutions. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
